title: Sisymbrium standleyi
taxonomy: Sisymbrium standleyi is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae. It belongs to the genus Sisymbrium, which consists of annual or biennial plants commonly known as mustard or bladderpod. This plant is native to Mexico, and its classification reflects its unique characteristics and growth habits within the Brassicaceae family. Sisymbrium standleyi is further categorized under the subfamily Brassicoideae, which includes many species of mustard and cabbage plants. Within the family Brassicaceae, Sisymbrium standleyi is a distinct species with its own set of identifying features.
